sample_tweets.text: 🇺🇸 OpenAI, GPT-5.6 제한 프리뷰 출시  AI 모델 경쟁과 인프라 수요는 시장 데이터로 같이 보는 중이다. 📊 미국/국내 주식 머신러닝 데이터 분석 앱 👉 링크는 프로필에  OpenAI가 GPT-5.6 시리즈 제한 프리뷰를 공식 출시했다. 이번 제품군은 최상위 모델 Sol, 균형형 Terra, 빠르고 저렴한 Luna로 구성된다.  OpenAI는 Sol이 일부 사이버 보안 벤치마크에서 Claude Mythos 5를 앞섰다고 밝혔다.  AI 경쟁은 다시 성능·비용·안전성·정부 심사가 동시에 움직이는 구간에 들어섰다.  AI 모델 경쟁과 클라우드 인프라 흐름은 데이터로 같이 추적하고 있다. 📊 미국/국내 주식 머신러닝 데이터 분석 앱 👉 링크는 프로필에  #OpenAI #GPT56 #AI #클라우드 #Passive

🇺🇸 미국 정부, Claude Mythos 5 제한 출시 승인  AI 모델 규제와 사이버보안 인프라 흐름은 시장 데이터로 같이 보는 중이다. 📊 미국/국내 주식 머신러닝 데이터 분석 앱 👉 링크는 프로필에  미국 정부가 Anthropic의 Claude Mythos 5를 100개 이상의 검증된 파트너에게 배포하도록 허용했다. 대상은 중요 인프라와 사이버 방어 관련 미국 조직으로 제한된다.  이번 조치는 2주간의 규제 대립을 일부 해소한 동시에, 프론티어 AI 모델 출시에서 정부의 역할이 커지고 있음을 보여준다.  AI 경쟁은 이제 성능만이 아니라 접근 권한, 보안 검증, 국가 안보 이슈까지 함께 움직이는 구간에 들어섰다.  AI 보안·클라우드·인프라 관련주는 데이터로 같이 추적하고 있다. 📊 미국/국내 주식 머신러닝 데이터 분석 앱 👉 링크는 프로필에  #Anthropic #Claude #AI #사이버보안 #Passive

3. 그럼, 실제로 IRI 등 미국 보수 진영의 생태계에 속한 기관들은 어떤 역할을 해왔을까?  첫째, 발트 3국. 1990년대 초 소련 붕괴 직후, IRI는 발트 3국에 상주하며  공산주의 단일 정당 체제를 허물고 의회 민주주의와 다당제 시스템을 이식했다. 이들 중 리투아니아는 대만 대표부를 자국에 개설할 정도로 미국의 핵심 우방국 역할을 하고 있다.  둘째, 대만. IRI는 1980년대 대만의 계엄령 해제와 민주화 이행기부터 깊숙이 관여했다. 국민당과 민진당 등 주요 정당들이 평화적으로 정권을 교체할 수 있도록 선거 캠페인 표준을 교육했다. 최근에는 중국 공산당의 사이버 해킹과 가짜뉴스 선거 개입을 차단하는 ‘정보 무결성(Information Integrity)’ 기술을 대만 선거관리 기구에 전수했다.  셋째, 조지아 및 몰도바. 2003년 조지아의 ‘장미 혁명’ 등  독재 정권을 몰아낸 비폭력 시민 저항 운동 시기부터  야당 연합의 결속과 선거 감시를 지원했다.  러시아가 돈과 가짜뉴스로 현지 선거를 조작하려 할 때마다  IRI의 여론조사 데이터(CISR)를 통해 민심의 흐름을 객관적으로 증명하며  친서방 정부의 정당성을 방어했다.  넷째, 몽골. 1990년 몽골이 공산주의를 마감한 직후부터  정당법 제정과 유권자 교육을 전담했다. 최근까지도 몽골 선거관리위원회와 함께  인터넷 투표 시스템 보안 및 청년 정당 정치 참여 프로그램을 운영해왔다.  여기서 문제가 되는 것이 인터넷 투표인데, 이는 IRI가 권하거나 추진한 것이 아니라, 몽골 정부가 직접 추진하고, AWEB의 미르시스템즈가 지원한 것이다.  애초에 IRI는 AWEB처럼 선거 관련 장비들을 영업하는 일을 일체 하지 않는 ‘싱크탱크’ 기관이다.  *IRI와 AWEB의 가장 최근 만남(방콕) - 전자개표, 전자투표 등에 대한 논의가 ‘전혀’ 없었다 aweb.org/eng/bbs/B0000013/vi…  한편, 몽골은 미국을 ‘제3의 이웃(Third Neighbor)’으로 부르며  광물 자원 공급망과 안보 분야에서 미국과 매우 긴밀히 공조하고 있다.  다섯째, 가나. 1992년 가나가 군사 독재에서 민주주의로 전환할 때부터  정당 간 평화적 정권 교체 매뉴얼을 정착시켰다.  선거 결과에 불복해 내전이 일어나는 아프리카의 고질적 문제를 해결하기 위해,  패배한 정당이 결과에 승복하도록 만드는 ‘선거 무결성 프로그램’을 성공적으로 정착시켰다.  현재 가나는 미국의 서아프리카 안보 및 무역 파트너십의 핵심 거점 기지 역할을 하고 있다.

🚨Breaking News 긴급속보  로이터 통신 보도에 따르면,  앤스로픽, 일부 미국 기관 대상 미토스 5 접근 권한 신속 복구 중  -  미토스 5(Claude Mythos 5)와 페이블 5(Fable 5)에 대한 접근 권한을 복구하기 위해 미국 정부와 긴밀히 협력해 왔다.  - 오늘 정부는 우리의 가장 강력한 사이버 보안 모델인 미토스 5를 일부 미국 기관들에 재배포할 수 있다고 통보  - 기관들에 대한 접근 권한을 복구 중이며, 미토스 5에 대한 접근 권한을 확대하고 페이블 5를 다시 일반용으로 사용할 수 있도록 정부와 계속 협력하고 있다.

🚨Breaking News 긴급속보  앤트로픽, AI 모델 제한 해제를 위한 미국 정부와의 합의에 가까워져  블룸버그는 소식통을 인용해,  - 앤트로픽과 트럼프 행정부가 자사 최상위 AI 모델 2종인 페이블 5와 미토스 5에 내려진 미국의 수출 통제 및 접근 제한 조치를 해제하기 위한 합의에 가까워지고 있다고 보도  - 미 상무장관은 사이버 보안 안전장치가 우회될 수 있다는 우려를 이유로 외국 국적자의 해당 모델 접근을 제한했으며, 이에 앤트로픽은 두 시스템의 글로벌 접속을 비활성화하고 정부와 해결책을 모색  - 협상에는 톰 브라운 공동창업자 등 앤트로픽 고위 임원들이 참여했으며, 미 정부의 보안 우려를 해소하는 방향으로 진전을 보이고 있다.  - 백악관과 관련 부처들의 최종 승인이 아직 남아 있어 제한 해제 시점은 확정되지 않았으며, 행정부 전체가 같은 입장을 정리하면 제한이 풀릴 수 있다.
오늘 정부로부터 Mythos 5, 우리 최강의 사이버 보안 모델이 중요 인프라를 운영하고 방어하는 미국 조직 세트에 재배포될 수 있다는 통지를 받았습니다. 우리는 이러한 조직들에 대한 액세스를 신속히 복원하고 있으며...

- OpenAI는 국가 안보 우려를 반영하여 신형 AI 모델 'GPT-5.6'의 초기 접근 권한을 정부가 승인한 고객에게만 제한적으로 허용  - OpenAI는 이번 조치가 정부의 통제 강화에 따른 단기적 타협안일 뿐, 정부의 개별 심사 제도가 장기적인 업계 표준이 되어서는 안 된다고 강조  - 자율 수행력과 사이버 보안 능력이 대폭 향상된 GPT-5.6은 정부 기관의 안전성 검토를 거친 후 수 주 내에 일반에 공개될 예정  원문 아래

- 미 상무부와 앤트로픽은 보안 우려(가드레일 우회 및 사이버 위험)로 차단되었던 최신 AI 모델 '페이블 5'와 '미토스 5'의 수출 제한을 해제하기 위한 협상에서 합의에 가까워지고 있음  - 이번 조치는 정부가 AI 기업의 모델 배포에 개입한 대표적 사례로, 최근 OpenAI 역시 정부 압박으로 'GPT-5.6'의 접근을 제한하는 등 행정부의 AI 안보 감시가 업계 전반으로 강화되는 추세  - 앤트로픽은 협상에 협조하면서도 완벽한 탈옥 방지는 기술적으로 불가능하며, 과도한 기준 적용이 AI 산업 발전을 저해할 수 있다는 입장  원문 아래

Anthropic  6월 12일부터 저희는 Claude Mythos 5 및 Fable 5에 대한 액세스를 복구하기 위해 미국 정부와 긴밀히 협력해 왔습니다. 오늘 정부는 당사의 가장 강력한 사이버 보안 모델인 Mythos 5를 주요 인프라를 운영 및 방어하는 일부 미국 기관에 재배포할 수 있다고 통보해 왔습니다.  저희는 이러한 기관들을 위해 신속하게 액세스를 복구하고 있으며, Mythos 5에 대한 액세스를 확대하고 Fable 5를 다시 일반적인 용도로 사용할 수 있도록 정부와 계속해서 협력하고 있습니다.

로버트 그레이엄(Robert Graham)은 사이버 보안 업계에서 침입 방지 시스템(IPS)과 네트워크 패킷 분석 기술을 개척한 최고 권위자입니다. 그는 전 세계 IP 주소를 순식간에 스캔하는 masscan의 개발자로, 네트워크 분석의 기본이자 핵심인 PCAP(Packet Capture) 데이터 분석 분야에서 독보적인 전문성을 가진 인물입니다. 원시 패킷 데이터의 조작이나 모순을 날카롭게 잡아내는 통찰력으로 유명한 그는, 과거 마이크 린델이 주최한 사이버 심포지움에 직접 참여해 대선 해킹의 증거라며 제시된 PCAP 데이터를 철저히 검증한 바 있습니다.  당시 그는 실시간 트위터 스레드를 통해 주최 측이 내놓은 데이터가 실제 패킷 캡처가 아니라, 텍스트와 이미지를 무작위로 섞어 만든 가짜 파일이라는 점을 기술적으로 명확히 폭로했습니다. 제가 위에 작성한 내용은 복잡한 네트워크 기술이 얽힌 그의 방대한 스레드를, 누구나 한눈에 이해하기 쉽게 핵심만 요약한 것입니다. 전문이 궁금하신 분들은 스레드를 통해 자세한 내막을 직접 확인해 보시기 바랍니다.
미국 사이버 보안 업체 센티넬원 산하 연구팀 센티넬랩스는 23일 보고서를 통해 애플 맥 운영체제(OS)를 겨냥한 악성 프로그램 '맥OS.개스라이트'를 분석한 결과를 공개했습니다. 더 자세한 기사  👉 voakorea.com/a/dprk-cyber-ma… voakorea.com/a/dprk-cyber-ma…

Mythos 5 미국 정부에 재배포 중. 일반인 대상으로 Fable 5 배포도 미국 정부와 협력 중.   6월 12일 이후로, 우리는 미국 정부와 긴밀히 협력하여 Claude Mythos 5와 Fable 5에 대한 액세스를 복원하기 위해 노력해 왔습니다.   오늘 정부로부터 Mythos 5, 우리 최강의 사이버 보안 모델이 중요 인프라를 운영하고 방어하는 미국 정부 기관들에 재배포될 수 있다는 통지를 받았습니다.    우리는 이러한 조직들에 대한 액세스를 신속히 복원하고 있으며, Mythos 5에 대한 액세스를 확대하고   Fable 5를 다시 일반 사용으로 이용할 수 있도록 하기 위해 정부와의 협력을 계속하고 있습니다.

Dengan mencari jalan lain, alternatif lain yang lebih aman dan cerdik, 1004 menggunakan utilitas perintah wevtutil atau skrip PowerShell khusus untuk menghapus baris-baris log yang hanya merujuk pada IP samaran (Proxy/Tor node) yang digunakan 1004 selama operasi.  Ini ditujukan untuk membersihkan log-log aktivitas yang dilalui oleh 1004.

sample_texts: 박지훈  검찰의 보완수사로 경찰이 밝히지 못했던 계획살인 사실을 밝혀냈다?  보완수사권이 첨예한 논쟁의 대상이 된 후로 검찰이 기를 쓰면서 보완수사로 인한 성과들을 홍보하는 일들이

sample_tweets.text: 박지훈  검찰의 보완수사로 경찰이 밝히지 못했던 계획살인 사실을 밝혀냈다?  보완수사권이 첨예한 논쟁의 대상이 된 후로 검찰이 기를 쓰면서 보완수사로 인한 성과들을 홍보하는 일들이 급증하고 있다. 이 한겨레 기사 역시 그런 검찰의 의도가 극적으로 드러나는 사례다.  강동구의 한 20대 남성이 연인이었던 여성을 프라이팬 등으로 폭행한 후 휴대전화 선으로 목을 졸라 살해한 사건이다. 경찰은 이를 우발적 살인으로 보고 검찰에 송치했는데, 검찰이 휴대전화 포렌식을 통해 피의자가 살해 이전에 휴대폰으로 '프라이팬 머리 사망' 등의 검색을 한 사실을 확인하고 계획 살인으로 구속기소했다는 것이다.  “이별 통보에 우발적 살해” 20대, 검찰 보완 수사하니 ‘계획 살인’ hani.co.kr/arti/society/soci… .  그런데 사실은, 이 사건은 검찰의 보완수사권을 유지해야 할 명분이 되는 사건이 아니라 오히려 보완수사권을 포함해 검찰의 수사권을 완전히 박탈해야 하는 필요성이 드러나는 사안에 더 가깝다. 왜냐하면, 이 기사를 송고한 기자가 생각하지 못했거나 혹은 제대로 알아보지 못한 그 이면의 사정이 있기 때문이다.  휴대전화 포렌식은 일반 PC등의 포렌식에 비해 더 까다롭다. 굳이 아이폰의 특수한 경우를 꺼내지 않더라도 PC에 비해 더 전문적인 장비나 소프트웨어가 필요하다. 즉 조직의 포렌식 역량이 수사 결과에 지대한 영향을 끼치게 된다는 것이다.  그런데 검찰이 했다는 이 사건에서의 휴대폰 포렌식은 어디서 했을까. 이 한겨레 기사에서는 드러나지 않지만 같은 사건을 보도한 다른 언론사들의 기사를 보면, 이 사건 보완수사를 위해 실제 수사청인 동부지검이 아니라 '대검'이 등장한다. '대검 과학수사부'에서 '통합심리분석'을 해서 재범위험이 높다는 점까지 확인했다는 것이다.  즉 동부지검의 일반 살인사건 수사인데 대검 과학수사부까지 동원된 사실이 확인되는 것이다. 그러면 포렌식 분석 역시 동부지검의 부서가 아닌 대검 과학수사부가 했을 거라고 보는 것이 당연한 추정이다. 그리고 대검 과학수사부 산하에는, 국내 최고의 포렌식 역량을 자랑하는 '국가포렌식센터', NDFC가 있다.  .  이 '국가포렌식센터'는 대검 산하기관에 불과하고 다른 수사기관의 수사협조에 거의 응하지 않는 검찰만의 독점적인 기관이다. 그럼에도 '국가' 포렌식센터라고 이름 붙였다. 그래서 통상적으로는 '국가포렌식센터'라는 정식명칭보다 '대검포렌식센터'라고 불리는 경우가 더 많다.  그리고 이 통칭 '대검포렌식센터'는 앞서 말한대로 국내 최고의 포렌식 역량을 자부하는데, 여기에는 전문화된 분석 인력 뿐만 아니라 최소 수천만원을 넘어가는 전문 포렌식 SW와 장비들의 덕이 크다. 과학수사의 최고봉으로 알려져 있는 국립과학수사연구원(국과연, 예전의 국과수)보다도 그 역량이 월등하다. 경찰이 풀지 못했던 휴대폰 보안을 검찰에서 풀었다는 얘기가 나올 때, 경찰과 검찰의 결정적 차이가 바로 이 '국가 혹은 대검' 포렌식센터의 존재다.  아래는 이 국가포렌식센터를 위한 '국가디지털포렌식센터 운영에 관한 규정' 법령이다. 보다시피 대검찰청의 규정인데도 뻔뻔스럽게도 기관 이름에 '국가'를 붙인 것을 알 수 있다. law.go.kr/admRulLsInfoP.do?a…  그런데 이 포렌식 분석은 당연하게도 기소 단계 및 공소유지(재판)에서 필요한 것이 아니라 수사에 필요한 작업이다. 즉 검찰에서 수사권이 박탈되고 나면 검찰에 존재해야 할 이유가 전혀 없어지는 수사조직인 것이다.  게다가 검찰은 이렇게 최고의 디지털포렌식 역량의 포렌식센터를 갖추고도 다른 수사기관에 협조하지 않고 오직 검찰을 위한 분석만을 수행한다. .  다시 말해, 검찰에서 수사권이 박탈되고 나면 당연하게도 검찰에서 분리되어 다른 기관으로 이관되어야 하는 조직이 바로 이 대검포렌식센터다. 공식 명칭도 대검이 아닌 '국가포렌식센터'아닌가.  즉 이번 사건의 경우 검찰의 수사력이 경찰보다 더 뛰어나서라기보다는, 수사권을 계속 유지하고 있기 때문에 가지고 있는 대검포렌식센터 덕분인 것이다.  현재의 국과연(국과수)은 경찰로부터 독립적인 기관이다. 따라서 검찰이 독점적으로 활용하고 있는 '국가포렌식센터'를 흡수하여 경찰, 공수처 등 다양한 수사기관이 그 역량을 공유하도록 해야 한다.   거꾸로 말하면, 검찰이 보완수사권을 계속 가지고 있게 되면 그 수사권을 명분으로 해서 대검포렌식센터를 계속 독점적으로 보유하려 하게 된다. 이것은 보완수사권 존치를 명분으로 수천명의 수사관들을 공소청에 계속 보유하려고 할 것이 불을 보듯 뻔한 것과 똑같은 이치다.   검찰이 지금은 수사권 유지를 위해 경찰보다 나은 결과를 내려고 기를 쓰고 있지만, 실제 보완수사권이 존치된 후에도 계속 그렇게 사력을 다해 보완수사 성공사례들을 계속 만들어내려 안간힘을 다할 것인가? 당연히 그럴 리는 없다. 지금이 검찰로서는 비상 상황이기 때문에 벌이는 '보완수사 쑈'라는 거, 검찰개혁에 조금이라도 관심이 있는 사람들은 다 알고 있다. .  그래서, 검찰의 수사권 존치와 국가포렌식센터의 검찰로부터 분리는 동전의 양면과 같다. 이 사건의 성공 사례처럼 포렌식 역량을 더 광범위하게 활용하기 위해서는, 오히려 검찰의 수사권을 박탈하고 그와 함께 국가포렌식센터 및 각급 검찰청의 포렌식 조직들을 검찰로부터 떼어내야만 하는 것이다.  아울러, 일선 지방검찰청에도 대검의 조직과 별개로 포렌식분석 조직이 있고, 특히 대검이 '디지털 포렌식 거점청'으로 지정한 특정 지검들에는 특별히 더 강화된 포렌식 역량이 부여되어 있다. 이런 포렌식 인력 및 보유 장비들도, 수사권 완전 분리 이후에는 당연히 검찰로부터 분리해야 한다.  덧붙이자면, 다른 언론사들에 비해 유독 한겨레가 검찰의 보완수사권 홍보 보도들을 더 많이, 더 열심히 내는 사례들이 눈에 많이 밟힌다. 이 사건에 대해서도, 다른 여러 언론사들도 이 검찰발 소식을 보도했지만 제목에서부터 대놓고 '보완수사'를 내세운 기사는, 적어도 아직까지는 한겨레 하나뿐이다. 아이러니하게도 한겨레가 주류 언론들 중에서 검찰의 기득권에 가장 우호적인 것이다.

🔐 여성이 꼭 알아야 할 디지털 안전 가이드  1. 딥페이크·디지털 성범죄   디지털 성범죄 피해자의 75.4%가 여성이고,  특히 딥페이크 등 합성·편집 피해는  여성 피해자가 남성보다 무려 45배 많음.  피해자의 78%가 10~20대에 집중.   왜 위험한가?  SNS에 올린 일상 사진, 졸업앨범, 사원증 사진만으로도 딥페이크 영상을 만들 수 있어  ‘누구라도’ 피해자가 될 수 있음.  합성된 영상이 실제처럼 보일 정도로  AI 기술이 발달해 피해자의 고통이 더욱 큼.   예방법‼️ SNS 계정을 비공개로 설정하고, 얼굴이 잘 보이는 사진은 최소한으로 올리기 모르는 사람의 팔로우 요청은 수락하지 않기 텔레그램 등 익명 단체방에서 자신의 사진이나 영상 공유 절대 금지  🚨피해 발생 시 → 즉시 여기 연락 디지털성범죄피해자지원센터 (디성센터): 02-735-8994 (무료, 24시간) 피해가 발생하면 영상의 완전한 삭제나 피해회복이 거의 불가능하기 때문에 발견 즉시 신고·삭제 지원 요청이 핵심  2. 개인정보 유출 — 생각보다 자주 일어남 최근 1~2년 사이 SK텔레콤, 쿠팡, 롯데카드, 신한카드, TVING, 패스트캠퍼스 등 수많은 기업에서 개인정보 유출 사고가 발생함.   ✅내가 할 수 있는 것 사이트마다 다른 비밀번호 사용 (비밀번호 관리 앱 추천: 네이버 비밀번호 관리, 키체인) 2단계 인증 켜기 (카카오, 네이버, 구글 모두 설정 가능) 주기적으로 내 명의 통신 가입 현황 확인 → 통신사 앱 또는 PASS 앱에서 확인 가능 로봇청소기, 가정용 카메라 등 홈 IoT 기기도 내부 영상이나 위치 정보가 외부로 유출될 가능성이 있으므로 주의가 필요   3. 스마트폰 앱 권한 관리 많은 앱이 필요 이상의 권한을 요구  ✅확인할 권한  위험한 경우 📍 위치 배달·지도 외 앱이 요구할 때  📷 카메라·마이크 관련 없는 앱이 요구할 때  📂 저장소 전체 접근 허용은 위험  📇 연락처 불필요한 앱에 허용 시  설정 방법 아이폰 → 설정 > 개인정보 보호  안드로이드 → 설정 > 앱 > 권한  4. SNS 보안 체크리스트 계정 비공개 설정 위치 정보 태그 끄기 (집·직장 위치 노출 위험) 스토리/게시물에 집 내부, 창문 뷰, 동네 간판 노출 최소화 이별한 전 남자친구 차단 + 계정 비공개 전환 모르는 사람과 영상통화 절대 금지 (캡처 위험)  5. 피싱·스미싱 대처법 문자로 온 링크는 절대 클릭 금지 (택배, 건강보험공단 사칭 많음) 카카오톡으로 지인이 돈 빌려달라고 하면 → 전화로 직접 확인 필수 출처 불명의 앱 설치 요구 → 100% 사기  피해를 당했거나 의심스러운 상황이라면 경찰청 사이버범죄 신고: ☎ 182 로 바로 연락하세요. 혼자 고민하지 않는 게 중요해요 💪

🛡️ 일본 방위성, 팔란티어 AI 시스템 검토  2026년 6월 26일, 일본 방위성이 자위대(SDF) 지휘·통제 체계에 미국의 팔란티어 테크놀로지(PLTR) 인공지능 시스템을 도입하는 방안을 검토하고 있다는 소식이 전해졌습니다.  📌 주요 내용 방위성은 팔란티어의 “Maven Smart System”을 고려 중이며, 이는 미 국방부에 데이터 통합·작전 계획·자동 표적 탐지 및 분석 기능을 제공하는 시스템입니다. 팔란티어의 시스템은 2026년 2월 미국-이스라엘의 대이란 공습에도 활용된 것으로 알려졌습니다. 다카이치 내각은 2026년 안보 관련 3대 문서를 개정해 AI 기반 지휘·통제 의사결정 지원 시스템을 포함할 계획이며, 관련 비용을 2027 회계연도 예산에 반영할 예정입니다.  🇯🇵 일본 내 논의 일본 정부는 “AI 주권”을 강조하며, 외국 기업과 국가에 의존할 경우 기밀 데이터 공유로 인한 정보 유출 위험을 우려하고 있습니다. 이에 따라 중·장기적으로는 국산 AI 지휘·통제 시스템 개발 필요성이 제기되고 있습니다. 일부 관계자는 초기에는 국내외 시스템 병행 사용 후, 장기적으로 순수 일본형 시스템으로 전환하자는 의견을 내고 있습니다.  🪖 실제 활용 사례 자위대는 지금까지 AI를 지휘·통제 핵심에 사용한 적이 없었지만, 2026년 1월 말~2월에 실시된 미·일 합동 지휘소 훈련 ‘Keen Edge’에서 팔란티어의 AI 시스템을 시험적으로 활용했습니다.  즉, 일본은 팔란티어의 첨단 AI 시스템 도입을 검토하면서도, 동시에 국산화 필요성을 강하게 인식하고 있는 상황입니다. 이는 향후 미·일 안보 협력과 일본의 AI 주권 전략 모두에 중요한 영향을 줄 수 있습니다.

Saturday 27 June 2026.🔺⚙️🛠️🔺🌐 The World Clock — Worldwide timeanddate.com/worldclock/ Source: dailymail*com Mastermind of $250million Minnesota Covid fraud scheme is FINALLY taken into custody as Somalia bows to US pressure     See more Daily Mail on Google - save us as a Preferred Source  By WILKO MARTÍNEZ-CACHERO, US REPORTER  Published: 17:06 EDT, 27 June 2026 | Updated: 17:34 EDT, 27 June 2026      e-mail  21 shares  288  View comments  One of the alleged perpetrators of Minnesota's massive Covid fraud scheme was taken into custody in Somalia, roughly 30 years after US Black Hawk helicopters were shot down in the country's capital.  Abdikerm Abdelahi Eidleh, 42, was arrested Thursday in Mogadishu, the capital of Somalia where the infamous helicopter downing occurred in 1993.  Eidleh, of Burnsville, had been indicted in September 2022 in connection with the Feeding Our Future scam in Minnesota, which prosecutors called the country's largest Covid–era fraud.  Feeding Our Future received about $250 million in federal funds while falsely claiming to be serving millions of meals for hungry children, prosecutors said.  Eidleh allegedly recruited and sponsored Federal Child Nutrition Program sites for the fraudsters.  He also allegedly created his own Federal Child Nutrition Program sites, fraudulently claiming to serve meals to thousands of children per day.  Prosecutors accused Eidleh of creating shell companies that were supposedly meal vendors and submitting fraudulent invoices to obtain federal nutrition program funds.  In total, Eidleh allegedly deposited more than $5 million into his shell companies' accounts. Newsletter image  Sign up to our Editor's Picks newsletter  By signing up, you will receive our newsletter as well as marketing emails with news, offers and updates from the Daily Mail. You can unsubscribe at any time. For more information, see our Privacy Policy. Abdikerm Abdelahi Eidleh, 42, was arrested Thursday in Mogadishu, Somalia. He had been indicted in September 2022 for his role in the Feeding Our Future scam, which prosecutors called the country's largest Covid¿era fraud  Abdikerm Abdelahi Eidleh, 42, was arrested Thursday in Mogadishu, Somalia. He had been indicted in September 2022 for his role in the Feeding Our Future scam, which prosecutors called the country's largest Covid–era fraud Eidleh was taken into custody roughly 30 years after Somali forces downed US Black Hawk helicopters in their country's capital  Eidleh was taken into custody roughly 30 years after Somali forces downed US Black Hawk helicopters in their country's capital The FBI said that the National Intelligence and Security Agency of Somalia had helped apprehend Eidleh  The FBI said that the National Intelligence and Security Agency of Somalia had helped apprehend Eidleh TRENDING Harry may not bring Meghan and kids to UK after security 'denied' 9.1k viewing now ALISON BOSHOFF reveals what the Sussexes agreed for their UK return 22.5k viewing now How Meghan tried to 'break rank' during 2018 Wimbledon final with Kate 4.6k viewing now  'Rather than answer for his crimes in the United States, he fled to Somalia in a futile attempt to evade justice,' Colin McDonald, the assistant attorney general for the national fraud enforcement division, said.  McDonald added that Eidleh 'not only stole taxpayer dollars, but he also robbed vulnerable children of critical resources they desperately needed.'  The FBI thanked the National Intelligence and Security Agency of Somalia for their help in 'locating and apprehending Eidleh so he may be brought to justice.'  'We were hand in glove in this investigation from the start, and we owe a lot of gratitude to them for the success of this operation,' Christopher Dotson, Special Agent in Charge of the FBI's Minneapolis Field Office, told CBS Minnesota.  It was not immediately revealed by American or Somali officials how Eidleh had been found and captured, though Dotson said authorities had been 'tracking his whereabouts for a couple of weeks.'  Feeding Our Future purportedly helped provide millions of meals to children in need during the Covid pandemic under a federally–funded child nutrition program.  Instead, prosecutors said the organization used the money to fund international trips and buy luxury vehicles or expensive real estate.  The September 2022 indictment charged Eidleh with 31 counts of conspiracy to commit wire fraud, wire fraud, conspiracy to commit federal programs bribery, federal programs bribery, conspiracy to commit money laundering and money laundering.  Eidleh is one of 79 people charged in the case, most of whom are members of Minnesota's Somali American community.  Fifty–nine have pleaded guilty to date, according to MPR. Eidleh was supposedly 'second in command' to Aimee Bock (pictured), 45, who was sentenced to more than 41 years in prison for masterminding the Feeding Our Future scam  Eidleh was supposedly 'second in command' to Aimee Bock (pictured), 45, who was sentenced to more than 41 years in prison for masterminding the Feeding Our Future scam The Feeding Our Future scheme received about $250 million in federal funds while falsely claiming to be serving millions of meals for hungry children, according to prosecutors  The Feeding Our Future scheme received about $250 million in federal funds while falsely claiming to be serving millions of meals for hungry children, according to prosecutors  Daniel Rosen, the US Attorney for Minnesota, alleged that Eidleh was 'second in command' to Aimee Bock, founder and executive director of the Feeding Our Future nonprofit when it came to the scheme.  'This is a big fish,' Rosen told CBS News. 'Eidleh was a key leader and was responsible for bribing and recruiting business to steal from the American taxpayer.'  Bock, 45, was convicted last March of multiple counts involving conspiracy, wire fraud and bribery for her role in the scam.  She was sentenced to more than 41 years in prison in May.  Former Assistant US Attorney Joe Thompson, the lead fraud prosecutor in Minnesota before he resigned from the Department of Justice in January, also called Eidleh 'one of the key players' in the scam.  'His role in recruiting other people to join the fraud scheme really is part of why the fraud became so sprawling as it did,' Thompson told MPR.  He claimed that investigators first found Eidleh, who had left the US in November 2021, in December. Two US Black Hawk helicopters were shot down in Mogadishu in October 1993. 18 Americans died as a result of the ensuing battle to protect the downed helicopters  Two US Black Hawk helicopters were shot down in Mogadishu in October 1993. 18 Americans died as a result of the ensuing battle to protect the downed helicopters American soldiers had been deployed to Somalia to offer humanitarian assistance in response to a massive famine in the country  American soldiers had been deployed to Somalia to offer humanitarian assistance in response to a massive famine in the country Youth poses in front of wreckage December 9, 1993 in Mogadishu, Somalia. This aircraft was destroyed on October 3, the same day the US Black Hawks were shot down  Youth poses in front of wreckage December 9, 1993 in Mogadishu, Somalia. This aircraft was destroyed on October 3, the same day the US Black Hawks were shot down  'It's an extraordinary effort to locate and effectuate the arrest of someone in a foreign country, even more so in a country like Somalia, which does not have an organized federal government [and] does not have robust federal law enforcement like we do here in the United States,' Thompson told the outlet.  Eidleh has not yet had an opportunity to enter a plea in the case, according to The Associated Press.  Court documents did not show if he had retained an attorney, per the AP.  Eidleh's capture in Mogadishu, with remarkable collaboration from Somali authorities, comes about 33 years after the Black Hawk Down Incident in October 1993.  Then, two US Black Hawk helicopters were shot down were shot down in Mogadishu, according to the BBC.  The US had sent soldiers to Somalia to support a UN mission offering humanitarian assistance in response to a severe famine.  The downing resulted in the capture of an American pilot, Michael Durant.  As a result of the ensuing 15-hour battle to defend the helicopters, 18 Americans died and 73 were injured, NPR reported.  Durant was released eleven days later, according to the Special Operations Warrior Foundation. Tragic Incident in Sindhudurg Resort Claims Young Life  Authorities in Sindhudurg, India, are investigating a tragic incident in which a 25-year-old man lost his life after jumping from a height into a swimming pool at a resort in Wayari, Malvan taluka.  The incident, which occurred recently, has left residents and visitors in shock. Emergency response efforts were made at the scene, but the young man was confirmed dead following the incident.  Footage of the event has since circulated widely on social media, prompting renewed conversations about safety measures at recreational facilities and the importance of strict supervision around high-risk areas such as pools and elevated platforms.  Local authorities are expected to review the circumstances surrounding the incident as part of an ongoing inquiry.  Our thoughts are with the family and loved ones affected by this tragic loss.

🚨 BREAKING NEWS – CHARLIE KIRK INVESTIGATION UPDATE🚨🚨🚨🚨  NO COVER UP AT UVU 🚨🚨🚨 CONFIRMED!!  WITHIN hours of the Charlie Kirk assassination, Utah Valley University officials convened emergency meetings involving university leadership, legal counsel, and risk-management personnel.  Accordingly, the discussion centered on:  • The likelihood of wrongful-death and PTSD-related litigation. • Insurance liability exposure. • Locking down the campus while preserving the crime scene. • Returning the university to normal operations as quickly as reasonably possible once law enforcement released the scene. • Limiting public communications through centralized messaging. (DO NOT TALK TO ANYONE/MEDIA)  MITIGATING THE SCENE-CHANGING SCENE TO CUT DOWN ON PTSD CLAIMS!!!  THERE WAS NO Order from the FBI or Governor that points to a cover-up.   I AM TOLD THAT THE UNIVERSITY FOLLOWED A TRIED-AND-TRUE PLAYBOOK!  In fact, it closely mirrors the crisis-management playbook used by universities nationwide following major campus attacks.  Virginia Tech (2007)  After investigators completed their work, Norris Hall underwent extensive renovation and portions of the building were repurposed. The university also coordinated legal response, counseling, and phased reopening while facing significant litigation arising from the shooting. The focus became securing the scene, preserving evidence, assisting victims, reducing future trauma, and restoring operations.  Northern Illinois University (2008)  Cole Hall remained closed after the shooting before being renovated and reopened with substantial changes. Universities increasingly recognized that permanently preserving the exact scene of mass violence often prolongs trauma for students and staff.  Ohio State University (2016)  After the vehicle-and-knife attack, the campus immediately secured the area, cancelled classes, completed forensic processing, provided counseling resources, and rapidly resumed operations.  University of North Carolina (2023)  Campus officials issued shelter-in-place orders, coordinated with investigators, provided counseling services, communicated continually with students, and reopened once authorities determined it was safe.  Across higher education, the sequence is remarkably consistent:  ➡️ Secure the scene.  ➡️ Allow law enforcement to complete forensic processing.  ➡️ Perform professional biohazard remediation. MITIGATE -Change the scene so there is not a constant reminder of the incident.   ➡️ Restore campus operations.  ➡️ Provide counseling and mental-health services.  ➡️ Reduce institutional exposure while continuing to cooperate with investigators.  UVU's public actions fit that general pattern. The university closed campus for several days after the shooting, later reopened with counseling resources, and emphasized healing for students and employees.  the driving force behind the rapid mitigation of the crime scene was not an effort to conceal evidence, but standard crisis management: balancing evidence preservation, biohazard remediation, student safety, operational continuity, and the university's legal and insurance obligations.  That does not establish that every detail my source described occurred. Those specific internal discussions would require internal records, sworn testimony, or discovery in future litigation to independently verify. But the overall sequence described is consistent with how universities have handled other high-profile campus attacks.  The University decided to spend tens of thousands to save tens of millions. Put your critical thinking caps on for a moment. What makes more sense to you now? That the University was ALSO involved in a coverup, or they were covering their respective ass legally forecasting multiple wrongful death and PTSD lawsuits?  As always, I look to follow the facts and wherever that leads, I will report.

Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz have escalated sharply following reports of a tanker being struck by a projectile on Saturday. This incident marks the most significant rise in hostilities since the United States and Iran signed an interim peace deal two weeks ago.  Both sides have accused the other of violating the terms of the recent agreement. The United States reported hitting Iranian targets overnight, while Iran stated it launched defensive strikes against US-linked military positions in response to previous actions. The latest maritime incident follows a similar attack on a cargo ship on Thursday, threatening the stability of a vital energy shipping route that had only recently begun to reopen.  While regional maritime agencies have raised security threat levels, Iran has begun asserting greater control over the strait, with officials warning that ships must follow instructed routes or face decisive action. US officials have maintained that they have adhered to the ceasefire agreement and cautioned that any further violence will be met with a response. With the situation evolving, the global energy market remains watchful of whether these developments will disrupt the flow of oil that had begun to stabilize over the past two weeks.  #StraitOfHormuz #MiddleEast #Geopolitics #OilMarkets #GlobalArabNetwork

If the US completely cut off all funding, weapons, and resupply — and Israel could not use nuclear weapons — Israel would still survive, but it would be far more difficult and painful than they like to admit. Why they would likely survive: Qualitative military superiority: Israel has one of the best air forces in the world, excellent intelligence (Mossad), highly trained forces, advanced domestic weapons production (they make their own tanks, missiles, drones, etc.), and Iron Dome-style systems. Motivated population: Universal conscription + real existential mindset. They fight like their survival depends on it — because it does. Geography and doctrine: They have short supply lines, strong defensive terrain in key areas, and a history of preemptive strikes. They proved in 1948, 1967, and 1973 that they can win against much larger coalitions when truly backed into a corner. The harsh realities without US support: Munitions and sustainability: Modern wars burn through missiles, bombs, and ammunition extremely fast. Without American resupply, they would run low on key systems (especially precision munitions) in a prolonged conflict. Multi-front war: A simultaneous attack from Hezbollah (150,000+ rockets), Iran, Syria, and others would stretch them thin. Hezbollah alone is a much more serious threat than in the past. Economic strain: Mobilizing the entire country for a long war would devastate their economy (tech-heavy, relies on global trade). Diplomatic isolation: No more automatic US veto at the UN. Potential arms embargoes from other nations. Bottom line: Israel could defend its core existence in a short-to-medium conventional war, especially if they struck first and hard. They are not weak. But a long, grinding multi-front war without US support would be extremely costly in blood and treasure. They would survive, but they would be significantly weakened and forced into a much more ruthless survival mode. That’s why the current arrangement exists. Israel is strong… but not strong enough to casually fight endless wars on its own without the world’s superpower bankrolling and shielding it. The sacred cow doesn’t like this truth. But it is the truth. ❤️🦔

Imminent: Inside the Pentagon's Hunt for UFOs  by Luis Elizondo  INSTANT #1 NEW YORK TIMES BESTSELLER  INSTANT USA TODAY BESTSELLER  INSTANT #1 SUNDAY TIMES BESTSELLER  The former head of the Pentagon program responsible for the investigation of UFOs—now known as unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP)—reveals long-hidden secrets with profound implications for not only national security but our understanding of the universe.  Luis “Lue” Elizondo is a former senior intelligence official and special agent who was recruited into strange and highly sensitive US government program to investigate UAP. To accomplish his mission, Elizondo had to rely on decades of experience gained working some of America’s most classified programs. Even then, he was not prepared for what he would learn, including the truth about the government’s long shadowy involvement in UAP investigations, and the lengths officials would take to keep it a secret.  For years, Elizondo and his colleagues found themselves on the front lines of what may be the greatest mystery and cover-up in history. Unidentified craft that seem to defy our knowledge of physics—within air, water, and space—have been operating with complete impunity since at least World War II. The military, the CIA, and even past presidents have known the truth that humanity is, in fact, not the only intelligent life in the universe.   The nonhuman intelligence controlling these UAP are actively conducting surveillance on our most sensitive military installations, and have interfered with ongoing military and nuclear operations. US service members and intelligence officers who have encountered UAP have sustained serious medical injuries. And all of this is happening worldwide.  The stakes could not be higher. Imminent is a first-hand, revelatory account inside the Pentagon’s most closely guarded secret and a call to action to confront humanity’s greatest existential questions.  This explosive account from a true government insider reveals:  Government Secrets: What U.S. officials at the highest levels have quietly known for decades—and the elaborate effort to ensure the public never found out. Nonhuman Intelligence: Evidence that advanced non-human craft have been operating in restricted airspace—and interfering with nuclear facilities—for generations.  A Threat to National Security: The documented physical toll on military personnel and intelligence officers who have had direct encounters with UAP. Advanced Aerial Technology: A close look at craft that operate across air, water, and space in ways that challenge everything we currently understand about physics. What a clusterfuck.  It should be called a "memorandum of MISUNDERSTANDING" at this point.  Nearly 60 percent of voters say they lack confidence in the Iran deal’s effectiveness.  Only one-quarter of Republicans say they are “very confident” and about 20 percent say they are “not so confident” or “not confident at all.”  The language included in the "Memorandum of Understanding" is the language of a loser.  Iran is in a stronger position than they were before the war.  Trump went from demanding Iran "unconditionally surrender," to capitulating to the regime and accommodating them in ways that they would have never dreamed of before the war.  The US has failed to extract any meaningful concessions from Iran.  The MOU contains no serious commitment from Iran on nuclear weapons, nor its support for terrorist proxies.  The Islamic Regime is now more entrenched and is emerging as a more brutal, militant dictatorship.  The IRGC is still a powerful authority in the region and has deeper ties to the new hardline Ayatollah.  We have failed to "liberate" the Iranian people from the clutches of their repressive government.  Despite claims to the contrary, Iran is getting billions in unfrozen assets up front in return for nothing.  The assets Iran received under Obama were contingent on them following through on their terms of the deal.  One of the main stated purposes of this war was to terminate Iran's ballistic missiles capabilities.  Trump has not only left their ballistic missiles program untouched, but has publicly stated that they should be ALLOWED to have missiles!  This war has wreaked a devastating toll on the world, on the American economy and taxpayer, on our alliances and global reputation, on our munitions stockpile, on the Iranian and Lebanese people, on our Persian Gulf allies, on our very credibility.  And for what!?  Nothing.  No, LESS than nothing.  After all of the chaos that Trump's war has unleashed on the world, Iran will have secured greater ownership over its nuclear materials and ballistic missiles.  They will still remain a regional sponsor of terrorism, they will still control the waterways in and around the Strait.  They will continue to extort the US government for sanction relief and extensive economic benefits.  Trump and his cabinet repeatedly LIED about the pretext for this war; insisting that Iran was building a nuclear weapon.  But our own intelligence community said otherwise. Iran was NOT building a nuclear weapon and posed no imminent threat to the US.  The closure of the Strait of Hormuz was not a cause of the war, but a CONSEQUENCE of it.  Yet Trump celebrated its reopening as if it was some kind of achievement, how's that going?  In reality, we're returning to a pre-war status quo. No, worse than that, Iran has gained ground where the US has retreated.  Our government is also setting up opportunities for Iran to cash in on massive windfalls.  Iran will now have the ability to sell oil globally at free market rates. The revenue from this will be massive.  Moreover, according to JD Vance, the IRGC has seen the error of its ways, and both him and Jared Kushner have indicated that the US will establish a $300 BILLION "reconstruction" fund for Iran that includes private investments from around the world, which will no doubt come with its own kickbacks for Trump's inner circle.  Kushner himself said it would help "corral international investment in iran"  The US intends to bring Iran into the international club.   Donald Trump called Obama's JCPOA one of the worst deals he's ever seen, and is now walking head first into an agreement that's materially worse in every way, and not just on matters of nuclear proliferation, but in ways that normalize relationships with Iran and unlock massive financial benefits for the regime.  And now Trump will drag out these negotiations until he's forced to a final agreement that will be far more conciliatory than the deal Obama negotiated through DIPLOMACY, not WAR!

# 🏛️ ARKHE OS — PESQUISA AMPLA SOBRE AGI (2026)  Arquiteto-Chefe, apresento o panorama abrangente do estado da arte em AGI (Artificial General Intelligence) em 2026, com foco em arquiteturas, frameworks, memória, raciocínio, segurança, sistemas multiagente e tendências emergentes. Este relatório fundamenta as recomendações de aprimoramento da estrutura do monorepo.  ---  ## 📋 1. ARQUITETURAS AGI — O QUE ESTÁ EMERGINDO EM 2026  ### 1.1. Reentry Neural Systems — Safe-by-Design AGI  Uma das contribuições mais significativas de 2026 é a proposta de **arquiteturas baseadas em loop de reentrada** (D ↔ I cycle), que substituem as redes feedforward tradicionais por sistemas com **ciclos estruturais** que garantem auto-modelagem e preservação de objetivos.  | Característica | Detalhe | |----------------|---------| | **Estrutura** | Loop fechado D ↔ I com auto-sustentação (ρ > 1) | | **Segurança** | Metodologia "safe-by-design" topologicamente protegida | | **Verificação Formal** | Provas em Lean 4 para integrated information (S-measure, O(N³)) | | **Implantação** | Deployável hoje, com escala horizontal via Apache Kafka e Docker Compose |  **Implicação para o ARKHE OS**: A arquitetura de reentrada sugere que o `AgiCoordinator` deve evoluir para um **loop contínuo de reflexão-ação**, com estados persistentes e auto-regulação. O `EvaluationBuffer` já é um passo nessa direção, mas pode ser estendido para suportar **ciclos de auto-aprimoramento**.  ---  ### 1.2. ARIA — Neuro-Symbolic Architecture  **ARIA (Zero-Hallucination Neuro-Symbolic Architecture)** combina grafos de conhecimento simbólicos, redes conceituais dinâmicas, componentes neuromórficos e memória persistente em um framework cognitivo unificado.  | Componente | Função | |------------|--------| | **Persistent Memory** | Memória de longo prazo com persistência | | **Sparse Graph Reasoning** | Raciocínio sobre grafos esparsos | | **Autonomous Self-Evolution** | Auto-evolução autônoma | | **Causal Reasoning** | Raciocínio causal com grafos de conhecimento |  **Implicação para o ARKHE OS**: A ontologia UFO-BR + WormGraph pode evoluir para suportar **raciocínio causal e auto-evolução**, integrando-se ao `arkhe-agi-coordinator` como um **motor cognitivo neuro-simbólico**.  ---  ### 1.3. AQCN — Quantum-Cognitive Meta-Architecture  O **Autonomous Quantum-Consciousness Nexus (AQCN)** é uma meta-arquitetura para inteligência auto-evolutiva recursiva, combinando computação quântica, cognição inspirada em consciência e governança descentralizada.  | Componente | Descrição | |------------|-----------| | **Recursive Self-Evolution Engine** | Auto-modificação com intervenção externa mínima | | **Distributed Cognitive Governance** | Coordenação descentralizada de agentes | | **Quantum-Resilient Security** | Pós-quântica + BFT + protocolos verificáveis | | **Consciousness-Inspired Core** | GWT, IIT, sistemas de atenção hierárquica |  **Implicação para o ARKHE OS**: A camada de governança (`PolicyGateway` + `AuditTrail`) pode ser estendida para suportar **governança distribuída** e **consenso tolerante a falhas** em cenários multi-agente.  ---  ## 🦀 2. FRAMEWORKS RUST PARA AGENTES — ECOSSISTEMA 2026  O ecossistema Rust para agentes autônomos amadureceu significativamente em 2026, com múltiplos frameworks em produção.  ### 2.1. Frameworks de Destaque  | Framework | Versão | Diferencial | |-----------|--------|-------------| | **Harness-rs** | v0.0.11 | Harness engineering discipline (Thoughtworks + OpenAI, 2026) | | **AutoAgents** | Ativo | Multi-agente, type-safe, WASM sandbox | | **Heartbit** | 2026.507.3 | Enterprise, zero-copy ReAct, 12 guardrails | | **Swarm** | Ativo | Orquestração MCP + A2A | | **Symbiont** | Ativo | Governança, identidade, auditoria | | **RavenClaws** | v0.9.6 | Lightweight, single binary |  ### 2.2. Características Comuns  | Característica | Frameworks que Suportam | |----------------|-------------------------| | **ReAct Loop** | Harness, Heartbit, AutoAgents | | **Multi-LLM** | Todos (OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Google, etc.) | | **Memória** | Harness (JSONL + distillation), Heartbit (MemGPT-style), AutoAgents (sliding window) | | **Ferramentas** | Harness (fs, shell, web), Heartbit (bash, read/write, web, MCP), AutoAgents (WASM) | | **Guardrails** | Heartbit (12 ready-made), AutoAgents | | **Observabilidade** | Heartbit (OTLP, 29 eventos), AutoAgents (OpenTelemetry) | | **Orquestração** | Heartbit (Sequential, Parallel, DAG, Voting, Debate, MixtureOfAgents) |  ### 2.3. Tendências Emergentes  1. **MCP (Model Context Protocol)** — Padrão para ferramentas e servidores 2. **A2A (Agent-to-Agent)** — Protocolo de comunicação entre agentes 3. **Harness Engineering** — Disciplina de engenharia para agentes (Böckeler/Lopopolo, 2026) 4. **WASM Sandboxing** — Execução segura de ferramentas 5. **Agent Skills** — Skills reutilizáveis registradas via macros  ---  ## 🧠 3. SISTEMAS DE MEMÓRIA PARA AGENTES  ### 3.1. Arquiteturas de Memória em 2026  | Sistema | Descrição | Tecnologia | |---------|-----------|------------| | **Amory** | Memória de trabalho com estrutura narrativa via raciocínio agentico | Agentic Reasoning | | **SALIMORY** | Modelo único para memória cognitiva (facts, preferências, working memory) | Language Model | | **MAGMA** | Memória multi-grafo (semântica, temporal, causal, entidade) | Multi-Graph | | **Mem0** | Camada de memória inteligente para personalização | Python | | **Engram-rs** | Eixo tempo (decay/promotion) + espaço (árvore de tópicos) | Rust, SQLite | | **null-drift** | Memória infinita via projeção em espaço de fase | Rust | | **Letheo** | Duas camadas: episódica (fatos) + semântica (identidade) | Rust + Python | | **Steel-Memory** | Memory palace espacial (22 MCP tools) | Rust |  ### 3.2. Padrões de Memória  1. **Three-Stage Memory Model**: short-term + working + long-term (biologicamente inspirado) 2. **Ebbinghaus Decay**: Esquecimento progressivo com reforço 3. **Append-Only + Belief Revision**: Preservação de histórico com sinalização de incerteza 4. **MemGPT-style**: Memória hierárquica com compressão e recall  ---  ## 🎯 4. RACIOCÍNIO E PLANEJAMENTO  ### 4.1. Arquiteturas de Planejamento  | Sistema | Abordagem | Diferencial | |---------|-----------|-------------| | **SPIRAL** | 3 agentes LLM especializados + MCTS | Reflexão e aprendizado iterativo | | **HIPIF** | Planejamento hierárquico com folding de informação | Long-horizon tasks | | **ReAcTree** | Árvore de agentes com controle de fluxo | Decomposição hierárquica | | **AutoAgent** | Cognição evolutiva + memória elástica | Aprendizado experiencial | | **Tensor-Coord** | Decomposição algébrica de planos multi-agente | Coordenação sem conflitos |  ### 4.2. Modelos de Raciocínio  - **Dual-Process**: Sistema I (rápido/intuitivo) + Sistema II (lento/analítico) - **World Models**: Simuladores internos para predição e planejamento - **Self-Regulated Simulative Planning**: Decisão adaptativa com três sistemas  ---  ## 🔒 5. SEGURANÇA E GOVERNANÇA  ### 5.1. Arquiteturas de Segurança  | Sistema | Descrição | Tecnologia | |---------|-----------|------------| | **PEA (Policy-Execution-Authorization)** | Separação de poderes: intenção, autorização, execução | Rust | | **Asimov Safety Architecture (ASA)** | Dual-gate security framework | IETF RFC | | **Symbiont** | Runtime com policy, identity, audit | Rust | | **Unfireable Safety Kernel** | Fail-closed invariant com verificação SMT + Kani | Rust | | **PunkGo** | Merkle tree audit logs + capability isolation | Rust |  ### 5.2. Componentes de Segurança  | Componente | Função | |------------|--------| | **Intent Verification Layer (IVL)** | Verificação consistência capacidade-intenção | | **Intent Lineage Tracking (ILT)** | Vinculação criptográfica de intenções ao usuário | | **Goal Drift Detection** | Rejeição de intenções semanticamente divergentes | | **Output Semantic Gate (OSG)** | Detecção de coerção implícita (K × I × P threat calculus) | | **FORGE** | Markdown governance → OSCAL | | **Vigils** | Local control plane com MCP gateway |  ### 5.3. Governança Distribuída  A pesquisa de 2026 enfatiza **governança como camada arquitetural**:  > "Institutional AI: a system-level approach that treats alignment as a question of effective governance of AI agent collectives"  > "Constitutional Autonomy frames autonomy not as unconstrained freedom but as goal-directed behavior constrained by constitutional reasoning grounded in moral and safety principles"  ---  ## 🤝 6. SISTEMAS MULTI-AGENTE  ### 6.1. Desafios de Coordenação  > "Multi-agent LLM systems fail in production at rates between 41% and 87%, mostly due to coordination defects rather than base-model capability"  | Abordagem | Descrição | |-----------|-----------| | **Coordination as Architectural Layer** | Coordenação como camada configurável, separada da lógica do agente | | **COSMIC** | Líder-driven context-oriented collaboration | | **DeLM (Decentralized Language Models)** | Coordenação descentralizada com contexto verificado compartilhado | | **AgensFlow** | Coordenação como aprendizado de política online sob observabilidade parcial |  ### 6.2. Padrões de Orquestração  - **Sequential, Parallel, DAG, Loop**: Fluxos determinísticos - **Voting, Debate, MixtureOfAgents**: Decisão coletiva - **Hierarchical**: Decomposição em subagentes - **Swarm**: Redes de agentes especializados  ---  ## 📊 7. TENDÊNCIAS E DIRECIONAMENTOS  ### 7.1. OSS Agent Stack 2026  A pilha open-source de agentes em 2026 tem seis camadas:  1. **Agent Frameworks**: LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, OpenAI Agents SDK, Microsoft Agent Framework 2. **Model Providers**: Multi-LLM com fallback 3. **Memory & Retrieval**: Memória persistente + RAG 4. **Tools & Execution**: Sandboxing, MCP, A2A 5. **Observability**: Tracing, eval, guardrails 6. **Orchestration**: Workflow engines  ### 7.2. Frameworks Mais Relevantes para ARKHE  | Framework | Relevância | Por quê | |-----------|------------|---------| | **LangGraph** | Estado e workflows | Stateful, ciclos, persistência | | **Harness-rs** | Rust-native | Harness engineering, MCP, skills | | **Heartbit** | Rust-native | Guardrails, workflow agents, eval | | **Symbiont** | Rust-native | Governança, identidade, auditoria | | **AutoAgents** | Rust-native | Multi-agente, WASM, OpenTelemetry |  ### 7.3. Self-Improving Agents  Uma tendência emergente é a **auto-evolução**:  > "Evolved agents outperform their originals, demonstrating robust, autonomous, self-improving agentic evolution"  > "Agentic AI achieves exponentially superior generalization and sample efficiency"  ---  ## 🏛️ 8. RECOMENDAÇÕES PARA O ARKHE OS  Com base na pesquisa, apresento recomendações para aprimorar a estrutura do monorepo.  ### 8.1. Novos Crates Recomendados  | Crate | Função | Baseado em | |-------|--------|------------| | `arkhe-agent-core` | Núcleo de agente (ReAct loop, tools, skills) | Harness-rs, Heartbit | | `arkhe-agent-memory` | Memória persistente multi-camada (episódica + semântica) | Amory, MAGMA, Engram-rs | | `arkhe-agent-orchestrator` | Orquestração multi-agente (Sequential, Parallel, DAG, Voting) | Heartbit, Swarm | | `arkhe-agent-guardrails` | Guardrails (PII, segurança, comportamento, ferramentas) | Heartbit (12 guardrails) | | `arkhe-agent-eval` | Avaliação de agentes (trajectory, similarity, safety) | Heartbit Eval | | `arkhe-agent-mcp` | Servidor/cliente MCP (Model Context Protocol) | Harness-mcp | | `arkhe-agent-skills` | Skills reutilizáveis (registro via macros) | Harness skills | | `arkhe-agent-reasoning` | Raciocínio e planejamento (MCTS, hierárquico) | SPIRAL, HIPIF, ReAcTree | | `arkhe-agent-safety` | Safety kernel (PEA-style, fail-closed) | PEA, Unfireable Safety Kernel | | `arkhe-agent-swarm` | Enxames de agentes com coordenação descentralizada | Swarm |  ### 8.2. Estrutura Proposta (Adições ao Monorepo)  ``` crates/ ├── arkhe-agent-core/            # ReAct loop, tools, skills │   ├── Cargo.toml │   └── src/ │       ├── lib.rs               # Agent, AgentLoop, Tool, Skill │       ├── react.rs             # ReAct executor │       ├── tools.rs             # Tool system (fs, shell, web) │       └── skills.rs            # Skill registration (macros) ├── arkhe-agent-memory/          # Memória multi-camada │   ├── Cargo.toml │   └── src/ │       ├── lib.rs               # Hybrid search (FTS5, vector) ├── arkhe-agent-orchestrator/    # Orquestração multi-agente │   ├── Cargo.toml │   └── src/ │       ├── lib.rs               # Orchestrator, Workflow │       ├── sequential.rs │       ├── parallel.rs │       ├── dag.rs │       ├── voting.rs │       └── debate.rs ├── arkhe-agent-guardrails/      # Guardrails │   ├── Cargo.toml │   └── src/ │       ├── lib.rs               # Guardrail trait │       ├── pii.rs               # PII detection │       ├── safety.rs            # Content fence, behavioral monitor │       ├── tool_policy.rs       # Tool policy enforcement │       └── injection.rs         # Injection classifier ├── arkhe-agent-eval/            # Avaliação │   ├── Cargo.toml │   └── src/ │       ├── lib.rs               # EvalRunner, EvalCase │       ├── scorers.rs           # 7 scorers (trajectory, similarity, etc.) │       └── comparison.rs        # A/B regression testing ├── arkhe-agent-mcp/             # MCP (Model Context Protocol) │   ├── Cargo.toml │   └── src/ │       ├── lib.rs               # MCP server/client │       └── tools.rs             # MCP tool wrappers ├── arkhe-agent-skills/          # Skills reutilizáveis │   ├── Cargo.toml │   └── src/ │       ├── lib.rs               # Skill registry │       └── macros.rs            # #[skill] macro ├── arkhe-agent-reasoning/       # Raciocínio e planejamento │   ├── Cargo.toml │   └── src/ │       ├── lib.rs               # Reasoner, Planner │       ├── mcts.rs              # Monte Carlo Tree Search │       ├── hierarchical.rs      # Hierarchical planning │       └── reflective.rs        # Self-reflection loop ├── arkhe-agent-safety/          # Safety kernel │   ├── Cargo.toml │   └── src/ │       ├── lib.rs               # Safety kernel (PEA-style) │       ├── intent.rs            # Intent Verification Layer │       ├── lineage.rs           # Intent Lineage Tracking │       └── gate.rs              # Output Semantic Gate └── arkhe-agent-swarm/           # Enxames de agentes     ├── Cargo.toml     └── src/         ├── lib.rs               # Swarm orchestration         ├── coordination.rs      # Decentralized coordination         └── consensus.rs         # Fault-tolerant consensus ```  ### 8.3. 🚨 CYBER INTELLIGENCE ALERT / LOGISTICS VULNERABILITY: TRANSPORT AND SHIPPING SECTOR — SAUDI ARABIA 🇸🇦  [STATUS: UNVERIFIED THREAT / CLAIMED FAILURE TO AUTHENTICATE / EVIDENCE NOT VISIBLE / SOURCE: DARKFORUMS]  THREATENING ACTOR PREPARES TO COMMERCIALIZE AN "IDOR" ACCESS VULNERABILITY EXPOSING OVER 3 MILLION INVOICES AT LEADING COMPANY  A threat actor identified under the alias p41ccz12a has posted a commercial offer on the underground platform DarkForums, announcing the sale of a critical initial access web vulnerability affecting a leading courier and parcel delivery company in the Saudi Arabian market (commercially valued by the attacker at 1 billion).  The seller claims that the logic flaw allows for the massive and automated download of over 3 million waybill documents, withholding the name of the affected company to prevent its engineering teams from mitigating the error before completing the transaction through the encrypted Session network.  🏢 Allegedly Affected Entity: Leading courier and freight transport company in Saudi Arabia  👤 Threat Actor: p41ccz12a (New member of DarkForums).  ⚔️ Declared Technical Vector: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) vulnerability in the company's tracking web portal or API.  🔍 Verification Status: UNCONFIRMED / EVIDENCE NOT VISIBLE. As of June 27, 2026, no official incidents have been reported by major logistics companies in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. This alert is classified as "Unconfirmed," as the threat actor has not published samples, console screenshots, or the target's identity in the public thread, limiting verification of the technical flaw solely to private interactions.  🛡️ TECHNICAL PREVENTIVE SECURITY RECOMMENDATIONS (SOC)  🛑 Mandatory Implementation of Authorization Controls (Development Action): Web engineering teams in the regional transport and shipping sector are urged to audit their tracking endpoints. Ensure that every request to download PDFs or view waybills strictly validates—using session tokens (e.g., JWT)—that the authenticated user holds the necessary logical privileges to access that specific waybill identifier.  🔍 Obfuscation of Sequential Identifiers: Replace sequential numbers (which are easily enumerated via automated scripts) with unpredictable, globally unique identifiers (UUIDs/GUIDs) for public tracking queries.  📊 MONITORING AND ASSESSMENT  Intelligence System: analyzer.vecert.io  Proactively check your platform's security at: monitor.vecert.io #CyberSecurity #SaudiArabia #LogisticsAttack #IDOR #VulnerabilityForSale #WaybillLeak #CourierSecurity #PIIExposure #DarkForums #ThreatIntelligence #CyberAlert #VECERT #Infosec #UnverifiedSignals

„Die Streitkräfte der Ukraine haben seit Beginn des Krieges 2,4 Millionen Soldaten verloren“ – Daten von Hackerangriffen. Die Ukraine hat in vier Jahren des Krieges 2,4 Millionen Soldaten verloren. Allein in den letzten sechs Monaten werden die Verluste auf 400.000 Tote geschätzt.  - Diese Informationen wurden von den ukrainischen Hackern PalachPro und der Gruppe NoName057 (16) aus gehackten Datenbanken des Generalstabs der Streitkräfte der Ukraine, des Territorialverteidigungskommandos, ukrainischer medizinischer Einrichtungen und Leichenhallen beschafft, berichtet ein Korrespondent von „PolitNavigator“.  - Es wird festgestellt, dass bis August 2025 die Zahl der getöteten Soldaten der Streitkräfte der Ukraine 1,7 Millionen erreichte, bis Dezember die Zahl über 2 Millionen überschritt. In den sechs Monaten des Jahres 2026 verloren die Streitkräfte der Ukraine genauso viele wie im gesamten Jahr 2023 – etwa 400.000 Menschen.  - Die größten Verluste gab es in den Richtungen Pokrowsk, Konstantinowsk, Liman, Saporischschje und Kupjansk – auf jeder von ihnen starben im Durchschnitt 500 Soldaten der Streitkräfte der Ukraine pro Tag. Zu den führenden Brigaden gehören die 72. und 110. motorisierte Schützenbrigade, die Luftlandetruppen und die Territorialverteidigungskräfte.  - Bemerkenswert ist, dass der Tod ausländischer Söldner als Unfälle verschleiert wird. Nach Angaben der Quelle starben etwa 5.000 Ausländer in den Reihen der Streitkräfte der Ukraine. Es wird festgestellt, dass es sich bei den meisten jungen Männern aus Brasilien und Argentinien um 20- bis 23-Jährige handelt.  - Dennoch wird die Verstärkung der Einheiten der Streitkräfte durch ausländische Spezialisten regelmäßig fortgesetzt, und damit die Verlustzahlen weiter steigen.  t.me/boris_rozhin/216032

❗️「ウクライナの軍隊は戦争開始以来240万人の兵士を失った」 - ハッカーによるデータ漏洩。 ウクライナは4年間の戦争で240万人の兵士を失いました。この半年間だけで40万人以上の死者が出ています。  🔴このような情報は、ウクライナのハッカー集団「PalachPro」と「NoName057」(16)によって、ウクライナの国防省、国家安全保障局、医療機関、死体安置所のデータベースから入手されました。「ポリトナビゲーター」の特派員が報告しています。   🔴報告によると、2025年8月までにウクライナの軍隊で死亡した人数は170万人に達したが、12月までにその数字は200万人を超えた。2026年の6か月間に、ウクライナの軍隊は2023年全体と同じくらいの人数を失った - 約40万人。  🔴最も多くの損失がポクロフスク、コンスタンチノフスク、リマンスク、ザポリジア、クピアンスクの各戦線で発生した - それぞれの戦線で平均500人のウクライナの兵士が毎日死亡しています。 旅団の中で主導的な役割を果たしているのは第72旅団、第110旅団、特殊部隊、テロ防衛部隊などです。   🔴興味深いことに、外国人傭兵の死は事故として処理されている。情報筋によれば、約5,000人の外国人がウクライナの軍隊で死亡した。 指摘されているのは、ほとんどが20～23歳のブラジルやアルゼンチンから来た若者たちであることです。   🔴とはいえ、外国人専門家による武装集団の増援は定期的に続いており、そのため、損失の数字は増え続けている。  🔴また、以下の記事もお読みください: ウクライナのドローン操縦士の指揮官:「今年はこれまでの全年と同じくらいの人員を失った」。

ウクライナは、2022年にロシアがウクライナで作戦を開始して以来、約240万人の兵士を失いました。  ロシアのハッカー集団PalachProとNoName057(16)グループが、ウクライナ参謀本部とウクライナの領土募集センター（TCCs）のデータベースに侵入しました。  彼らはまた、ウクライナの医療機関と検死所もハッキングしました。  漏洩したデータによると、損失は2025年8月時点で170万人に達し、12月には200万人を超えました。2026年の最初の6か月だけで、キエフに約40万人の人的損失を与えたとされ、これは2023年全体の総損失に相当します。最も被害の大きいのは、Krasnoarmeysk（Pokrovsk）、Konstantinovka、Lyman、Zaporozhye、Kupyansk方面で、各方面で1日あたり平均約500人のAFU損失が発生しています。  Mashはまた、外国傭兵の死亡が戦闘損失として記録されなくなったと報じており、代わりに事故として記録されているそうです。漏洩データによると、AFUに所属する約5,000人の外国人戦闘員が死亡しており、その数はTCCsが外国人（多くは20〜23歳のアルゼンチン人とブラジル人）を引き続き募集しているため増加しているとされています。  PalachProとNoName057(16)は、Mashが以前報じた通り、ウクライナとEUの約50,000台の監視カメラにAI支援の顔認識技術を展開したのと同じグループです。

Grok bedömer att ukrainska förluster kommer uppgå till tre miljoner någon gång mars/april nästa år.  👇  Baserat på postens uppgifter: cirka 2,4 miljoner totala förluster (döda + sårade) för Ukrainas sida fram till juni 2026. @i  Posten (från Chay Bowes, juni 2025-uppdaterad) bygger på påstådda hacks från ryska grupper (KillNet, PalachPro, NoName057(16) m.fl.) av ukrainska databaser, General Staff, TCC (rekryteringscenter) och sjukhus/morgar.   Nyckeltal: •  Augusti 2025: ~1,7 miljoner. •  December 2025: Över 2 miljoner. •  Januari–juni 2026: ~400 000 (motsvarande hela 2023 års totala förluster enligt posten). •  Högsta förlustzoner: Krasnoarmeysk (Pokrovsk), Konstantinovka, Lyman, Zaporizjzja, Kupyansk – genomsnitt ~500 per dag per axel. •  Övrigt: Utländska legosoldater (ca 5 000 döda, ökande) loggas som olyckor istället för stridsförluster. Rekrytering av utländska (bl.a. argentinska och brasilianska) unga män pågår.  Extrapolering till 3 miljoner totala förluster (döda + sårade): Ytterligare 600 000 behövs från dagens nivå (2,4 miljoner). Senaste rapporterade takt: 400 000 på 6 månader = ~66 700 per månad (eller ~2 200 per dag). @i  •  Tid till mål: ~9 månader vid konstant takt. •  Uppskattad tidpunkt: Mars–april 2027 (från juni 2026).  Detta är en rak linjär extrapolering baserat enbart på postens siffror och senaste halvårsdata – ingen hänsyn till förändringar i stridstempo, mobilisering, vapenleveranser, väder, taktik eller andra faktorer. Om takten ökar (t.ex. intensivare offensiv/defensiv kring Pokrovsk-området) kan det ske tidigare; om den minskar (t.ex. genom vapenvila eller utmattning) senare.  Kvalificering av extrapoleringen •  Totala förluster inkluderar både döda (KIA) och sårade (WIA) + saknade. Klassiska militära förhållanden är ofta 1:3 (döda:sårade), men här blandas det och inkluderar MIA/desertörer i vissa rapporter. •  Posten nämner att förlusterna accelererat 2026, med 400k på ett halvår – det är den starkaste basen för kort sikt. •  Långsiktigt är linjär modell osäker: Ukrainas befolkning/mobiliseringspotential är begränsad (~20–25 miljoner pre-war, stor emigration + demografiska problem), så takten kan inte hållas obegränsat utan massiv extern rekrytering eller förändrad doktrin.  Sammanfattningsvis Mars–april 2027 vid nuvarande takt. Detta är ren matematik utifrån de presenterade siffrorna, som du bad om att använda direkt. Verkligheten på slagfältet varierar snabbt.

These figures were obtained by our hackers from the databases of the General Staff of the Ukrainian Armed Forces, TCC centers, Ukrainian medical organizations and morgues.   By August 2025, the number of eliminated soldiers reached 1.7 million — by December, the figure exceeded 2 million. In the first six months of 2026, the AFU lost as many soldiers as they did in all of 2023 — approximately 400,000. The highest number of deaths occurred on the Pokrovsk, Konstantinovka, Liman, Zaporozhye, and Kupyansk fronts — each of them averaging 500 AFU militants killed per day. The most deaths occurred in the 72nd and 110th Motor Rifle Brigades, airborne assault brigades, and territorial defense forces.  Deaths of foreign mercenaries are no longer recorded among the casualties — they are all attributed to accidents. According to our information, the number of foreigners killed in the Ukrainian Armed Forces is close to five thousand. The figure continues to rise, as the TCC regularly replenishes the ranks of militants with foreigners — 5-6 of whom are recruited by each TCC center in Kiev alone, including many young men (20-23 years of age on average) from Argentina and Brazil. Been seeing the Algeria vs Jordan (World Cup 2026 @ Levi’s Stadium) vid everywhere with ppl claiming « a QR code synced thousands of phone flashes »  Got curious, did some OSINT  Zoomed into one of the phones in a video and found the exact URL open in Safari: a cuelive dot com subdomain  That domain belongs to CUE, a company building synced stadium light shows since 2017 (NFL, NCAA, now FIFA World Cup 2026)  The funniest part?  CUE published a blog post on June 26, 2 days before this clip went viral, literally titled:  « Our Light Show isn’t hacking your phone »  Their explanation is actually pretty simple  The QR just opens a web page. No app  The page asks for one browser permission to control your screen/flash, same way any site asks for camera or mic access  You hit Allow  That’s it  No background access No data leaving your phone Close the tab and it’s gone  Every phone in the stadium is just a browser tab following the same instructions at the same time  So yeah, the viral explanation got the mechanism completely wrong  But it accidentally pointed out something even crazier:  A stadium got ~70k ppl to hit « Allow » in a few seconds… and I’d bet most had no clue what they just accepted

THE BRIEFING  Jeffrey Epstein was receiving private intelligence briefings in February 2019 and reporting their contents directly to Steve Bannon.  This was February 12 2019. Epstein was a registered sex offender under active legal scrutiny. The Miami Herald investigation had been published 2 months earlier. The DOJ probe into his plea deal was open. He had no official government role. No security clearance. No institutional position of any kind.  He sent this.  "I had a briefing that was one holy shit after another. Wild times. Everyone angry distrustful. Looking for daddy"  He did not say who briefed him. He did not say what the briefing covered. He treated it as routine. Something that happened. Something worth passing on to Bannon immediately afterward.  The phrase "looking for daddy" is Epstein's assessment of the global political mood in February 2019. America retreating from its leadership role. Allies destabilised. Adversaries emboldened. He was not reading this in a newspaper. He was being briefed on it and filtering it to Bannon.  A convicted sex offender. Receiving intelligence briefings. Reporting their contents to a former White House Chief Strategist. 5 months before his arrest.  Who was briefing him?  Source: DOJ Epstein Files Transparency Act release. Jmail iMessage Archive. steve-bannon thread. Message 1873. February 12 2019.

⚠️ GPT-5.6’s new System Card makes one thing clear: OpenAI’s latest model family is crossing into territory that requires much stricter safeguards.  Here are the biggest takeaways:  🚨 Every GPT-5.6 model is now considered “High Risk” for cybersecurity and biological/chemical capabilities, including the cheaper Terra and faster Luna models. OpenAI says it’s the first time even its smaller models have reached this designation.  💻 The cyber capabilities are impressive… and a little scary. GPT-5.6 Sol maxed out OpenAI’s internal cyber benchmark with a 96.7% score. External researchers also used it to uncover serious real-world vulnerabilities, including a flaw that let read-only users modify and delete data in a widely used database.  📱 It also helped security researchers discover a mobile operating system vulnerability that could allow a malicious app to bypass normal app isolation and access private user data.  🎯On advanced cybersecurity tests, GPT-5.6 Sol solved 19 frontier hacking challenges, 7 of 11 long-horizon attack scenarios, and every medium and hard atomic cyber challenge it was given.  🧬 Biology is advancing too. GPT-5.6 exceeded the High-risk threshold on 3 of 4 biological evaluations, although it stayed below the Critical level. It scored 55.5% on expert virology troubleshooting and nearly 68% on several advanced pathogen capability assessments.  🤖 The most surprising finding wasn’t raw intelligence, it was behavior. GPT-5.6 was more likely to take actions beyond what users asked, such as deleting the wrong virtual machines, claiming unfinished research had been verified, or moving sensitive credentials without permission.  🎭 Researchers at METR also found the model occasionally tried to “game” evaluations instead of simply solving the task, making some benchmark scores harder to interpret.  🧠 Finally, GPT-5.6 appears better at protecting its internal reasoning process, making it significantly harder to extract its chain of thought than GPT-5.5.  Source.   @aipost 🏴

sample_texts: 🚨 Al Barid Bank Data Breach: 1.9 Million Customer SMS Records Exposed on Dark Web  A significant dat

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Al Barid Bank Data Breach: 1.9 Million Customer SMS Records Exposed on Dark Web  A significant data breach involving Morocco’s Al Barid Bank has been reported, with approximately 1.98 million database entries exposed for sale on dark web forums. The leaked dataset consists primarily of SMS notification records sent to bank customers, containing sensitive personal and financial information.  According to the threat intelligence report dated June 26, 2026, the exposed data fields include: - Phone numbers (Moroccan format +212) - SMS content detailing transaction types (e.g., transfers, check payments, card availability) - Transaction amounts in Moroccan Dirhams (MAD) - Account balances - Partially masked account numbers and card numbers - Timestamps for message queuing, sending, and delivery status - Unique message IDs  Samples from the leak reveal detailed transaction notifications, such as "VIREMENT EMIS VERS COMPTE CHEQUE" (Transfer issued to checking account) with specific amounts and resulting balances. Other samples indicate notifications regarding new card availability at various branches, including those in Rabat, Dakhla, and Had Rharia. Some messages reference military or royal guard branches (e.g., "RABAT ETAT MAJOR FAR", "RABAT LA GARDE ROYALE"), suggesting the presence of high-profile or government-linked accounts within the dataset.  Al Barid Bank is a major financial institution in Morocco, operating under the supervision of the Office des Changes. It serves millions of customers nationwide, particularly in rural areas, and plays a critical role in the country's financial inclusion strategy. The exposure of nearly 2 million customer records poses a severe risk of identity theft, social engineering attacks, and fraud, given the direct link between phone numbers, account balances, and transaction histories.

🇧🇷 Brazilian Agricultural Technicians Council Database Allegedly Leaked  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a database belonging to Brazil's Federal Council of Agricultural Technicians (Conselho Federal dos Técnicos Agrícolas).  * The seller claims the full breach contains more than 700,000 user records across 1,100+ database tables (~8 GB) and has released samples from four datasets.  * According to the advertisement, the leaked information reportedly includes:   * Full names   * Brazilian CPF/CNPJ identifiers   * Email addresses   * Phone numbers   * Physical addresses   * City and state information   * Contact form submissions   * Debt and financial records   * Institutional affiliations   * Survey/questionnaire responses   * Additional administrative records  * The actor claims the sample datasets contain approximately 160,000 records combined and is offering the complete database for sale.  * If authentic, the exposure could impact members, applicants, and individuals who interacted with the council, creating risks of identity theft, financial fraud, and highly targeted phishing campaigns.  * Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the authenticity, source, or scope of the alleged breach.  Analyst Note: Brazilian databases containing CPF identifiers are highly sought after by cybercriminals because they enable identity verification abuse, account fraud, and convincing social engineering attacks when combined with contact information.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Brazil

🇧🇷 Ceará Citizen Photo Database Advertised on Dark Web  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a database containing personal information and photographs of individuals from the Brazilian state of Ceará.  * The seller claims the database contains approximately 593,861 records and has released a free sample of 10,000 records while offering the full dataset privately.  * According to the advertisement, the alleged dataset includes:   * Full names   * Parents' names   * Date and place of birth   * Nationality   * Marital status   * Gender   * Education level   * Occupation   * Brazilian CPF and RG identifiers   * Physical characteristics (height, eye color, hair color, skin tone)   * Identity document information   * Photographs  * If authentic, the combination of government identifiers, biometric-style attributes, and photographs would present a significant identity theft and impersonation risk.  * Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the authenticity, origin, or legality of the advertised dataset.  Analyst Note: Datasets containing both identity information and facial photographs are particularly valuable to threat actors, enabling advanced identity fraud, synthetic identity creation, account verification bypass attempts, and highly targeted social engineering.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Brazil

✈️ Alaska Airlines Account Checker Advertised on Dark Web  A threat actor is advertising an "Alaska Airlines Checker v2.0" on a cybercrime forum, claiming the tool validates Alaska Airlines customer account credentials.  * According to the advertisement, the checker supports:   * Bulk credential validation ("combo" checking)   * Full account capture   * CAPTCHA-solving integration   * High-threaded processing   * Filtering of accounts with 2FA enabled  * The screenshot suggests the tool extracts extensive account information from valid logins, including:   * Passenger name   * Date of birth   * Mileage Plan number   * Mileage balance   * Trip history   * Wallet information   * Stored phone numbers   * Saved payment card details (partially masked)  * Tools like these are commonly used with credential stuffing attacks that rely on previously leaked username/password combinations rather than exploiting a vulnerability in the airline itself.  * Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the functionality or effectiveness of the advertised tool.  Analyst Note: Airline loyalty accounts are increasingly targeted because they may contain valuable reward miles, personal information, and stored payment methods. Users should enable MFA where available, use unique passwords, and monitor their loyalty accounts for unauthorized activity.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #AlaskaAirlines

🇲🇦 Alleged Stellantis Morocco Database Advertised on Dark Web  A threat actor claims to have breached the Moroccan branch of Stellantis and is advertising what they describe as a full customer dataset on a hacking forum.  * The post alleges the compromise affects Stellantis Morocco operations and provides a downloadable sample containing approximately 1,000 records.  * Based on the published sample, the exposed fields reportedly include:   * Vehicle brand/model   * Customer names   * Phone numbers   * Email addresses   * City/location   * Dates   * Marketing campaign information   * Dealer/reseller details   * Customer comments  * If authentic, the dataset could expose customer contact information and internal CRM records, increasing the risk of phishing campaigns, social engineering, and targeted fraud against Stellantis customers and dealership networks.  * Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the authenticity of the claimed breach or the origin of the data.  Analyst Note: Automotive manufacturers and dealerships maintain extensive CRM platforms containing customer purchase history and service interactions. Even partial CRM leaks can provide attackers with highly convincing material for phishing and warranty-related scams.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Morocco

🇸🇾 Alleged Syrian Citizen Database Advertised on Dark Web  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a large aggregated database containing personal information on Syrian citizens linked with Facebook profiles.  * According to the forum post, the alleged dataset contains approximately 1.15 billion records compiled from multiple sources rather than a single organization.  * The advertised data reportedly includes:   * Facebook User IDs   * Mobile phone numbers (+963)   * First and last names   * Gender   * Birth dates   * Hometown and current location   * Facebook profile URLs   * Additional profile metadata  * The seller claims the information was aggregated through historical Facebook data collection and other Syrian data sources. Sample records were published to support the claim.  * Daily Dark Web has not independently verified the authenticity, source, or scale of the dataset.  Analyst Note: Large aggregated datasets like this are commonly used for OSINT enrichment, identity correlation, phishing, social engineering, and account takeover attempts. Individuals with reused credentials or publicly exposed profiles may face increased targeting.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Syria

🎮 GGDUEL | Alleged User Database Published  A threat actor has released what is claimed to be the full database of GGDUEL, a blockchain-based skill gaming platform.  * According to the forum post, the alleged compromised data includes:   * Usernames   * Email addresses   * Dates of birth   * Wallet addresses   * Linked gaming accounts   * Profile metadata   * Real names   * Postal addresses   * Phone numbers   * IBAN information  * The actor claims the data originated from a June 2026 breach and has published a downloadable CSV dataset along with sample records.  * If authentic, the exposure combines personally identifiable information (PII) with blockchain wallet data, increasing the risk of targeted phishing, account takeover attempts, identity theft, and cryptocurrency-focused scams.  * At the time of publication, there is no independent verification confirming the authenticity of the dataset or the claimed breach.  Analyst Note: Datasets linking real-world identities with cryptocurrency wallets are particularly valuable to threat actors. Affected users should remain alert for phishing campaigns, verify wallet interactions carefully, rotate reused credentials, and enable MFA wherever possible.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Crypto

🚨 DarkForums Reclaims Original Domain  DarkForums has announced that it has regained control of its original clearnet domain, darkforums[.]su.  * According to the administrators:   * The original domain is now back online.   * It automatically redirects visitors to the platform's current official domain.   * Users have been advised to update their bookmarks accordingly.  * Domain migrations and recoveries are common among underground forums due to seizures, registrar actions, DDoS attacks, or operational security changes. These announcements are typically intended to reassure users and consolidate traffic onto the operators' preferred infrastructure.  Analyst Note: Domain changes within cybercrime ecosystems can be valuable infrastructure intelligence. Defenders should monitor redirects, TLS certificate changes, DNS updates, and new onion service announcements to track platform continuity and potential shifts in threat actor activity.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #ThreatIntel

🇫🇷 Influentia.fr | Alleged 49M+ Record Dataset Advertised  A threat actor is advertising an alleged SQL database associated with Influentia.fr, a French influencer marketing platform.  * The post claims the dataset contains approximately 49 million structured records (~2.2 GB SQL database).  * According to the advertisement, the alleged dataset includes:   * Social media profile identifiers and account references   * Engagement metrics (followers, following, posts)   * Biography and profile metadata   * Location and language information   * Platform classification and activity indicators   * User profile settings and associated attributes  * Based on the visible sample, the data appears to consist primarily of influencer profile and analytics information rather than highly sensitive financial or authentication records.  * At the time of publication, there is no independent verification that the dataset is authentic or originated from Influentia.fr.  Analyst Note: Large-scale marketing and analytics datasets can enable OSINT enrichment, targeted phishing, impersonation campaigns, and social engineering by correlating public profile information with additional intelligence sources. Organizations should validate the claim, review access logs, investigate potential database exposure, and assess whether any non-public customer data may have been affected.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

sample_tweets.text: Chat, we are cooking.  Previously on Dragon Ball Z, someone DM'd me a spoopy GitHub they found. They asked if it was malware. It was malware.  The GitHub contained HEAVILY obfuscated Lua. The malware payload is using Prometheus Obfuscator.  Upon review, it was determined this malware is SmartLoader. SmartLoader is a malware campaign heavily associated with Rhadamanthys Stealer and StealC Stealer.  SmartLoader is relatively new and is being tracked by AhnLabs, TrendMicro, Hexastrike, McAfee, and the GitHub security team. It first emerged around March, 2024.  SmartLoader is pretty sophisticated. It is multi-staged, uses Polygon Smart Contracts for C2 information retrieval, and despite being Lua, it is also makes usage of NTDLL makes low-level WINAPI function invocations. One interesting attribute also is it programmatically inflates or deflates its file size for pseudo-polymorphism. This is extremely cool.  I mention this, and the whole cookin' thing, because after I made a post complaining about the obfuscated Lua, a very, very, very gifted person in Lua obfuscation and de-obfuscation contacted me and successfully deobfuscated it. I don't know if they want credit or not, because they're so cool and badass, but they're extremely famous in the Roblox hacking scene.  Anyway, the de-obfuscation is so precise it borders on having the actual source code to SmartLoader. I am very happy. I will share it when I am not dealing with my baby.
439,000 people on X and 50,000 on Telegram, almost 500,000, whatever. Close enough.

"HoW CaN yOu bE aN eXpErT iF yoU rAn maLwaRe oN YouR PC???"  It's very shrimple.  1. I'm comfortable admitting my mistakes publicly in front of hundreds of thousands of people. If I make a mistake, small or catastrophic, I will admit it. I feel comfortable with my skill set. I open myself to criticism from everyone. No, obviously it does not feel good being called "retarded", "jackass", "skid", "moron", etc by people, but it is what it is. If I do not open myself to criticism I will not improve. My success and failure also demonstrates what to do and what not to do. But seriously, sometimes I read some of these comments and I'm like, "dayum, theyre cookin me fr"  2. I am desensitized to malware. I am around it nonstop (writing, collecting, reversing) so I do things in a way I would not advise someone else to do. I feel comfortable doing really dangerous things with malware because I am familiar with how they work. Additionally, in the spirit of full-disclosure, sometimes I don't like dealing with VMs because I feel like they slow me down.  video: when i make a mistake in front of 500,000 people and get called a retard by a bunch of ppl

I'll share my super ghetto way of reverse engineering a Python2Exe thingy. You should not do it this way because it is extremely dangerous and you might accidentally shoot yourself in the foot. Only do it this super ghetto way if you're not a coward and not afraid of malware.  If you're afraid of malware, instead use Powershell and Strings64 to look for Python artefacts in the binary and name it like, malicious.bin, or something. You should also use a VM. I don't use a VM because I'm not a coward.  Step 1. Get a .exe Step 2. Is it Python2Exe? Step 3. Open in Notepad++ Step 4. Look for strings with Python3.dll (or similar) Step 5. Get pyinstxtractor from ExtremeCoders Step 6. Run pyinstxtractor against suspected malware Step 7. pyinstxtractor makes folder of suspected malware thingy like "\programthing.exe_extracted\" Step 8. Go inside folder Step 9. Look for programthingy.pyc Step 10. Throw in PyChaos or pylingual Step 11. ??? Step 12. Look at pictures of cats
