Thank you to everyone who sent me free malware. I really appreciate it. When I am tired of writing malware or collecting malware, it is always fun to reverse engineer malware. My reverse engineering is meh, so it's good practice.  Please keep giving me free malware. I love you.

It's time to review some of the free malware people sent me in DMs.  tl;dr tl;dr - 1 no malwares - 1 dead malwares - 2 meh malwares - 3 good malwares  tl;dr - not malware, kid wanting ppl to review his minecraft mod - dead malware :( - SalatStealer, accidentally shot myself - MacOS malware, MacSync or something, idfk - weird malware never seen before, cool but vibe coded - new FAUX#ELEVATE malware campaign (good malware) - GachiLoader, KidKaki loader, Rhad campaign (very good malware)  non-tl;dr - Person sends me spoopy files they got on Discord. It was not malware. It was someone wanting legitimate feedback on their Minecraft mod stuff (surprising)  - Person links me to ClickFix website, the compromised website still has ClickFix active (social engineering to deliver malware). However, the website C2 (where the malware comes from) is dead. No free malware.  - Person sends me spoopy .exe they found. I download it, try to rename it, accidentally detonate it on my personal computer. It was SalatStealer. I had to disconnect my internet stuff, remove the files and persistence stuff it setup, and change my passwords. Very cool  - Person sends me weird GitHub link. It was multi-staged and obfuscated gunk. It ultimately delivered malware designed for MacOS. I don't do MacOS malware. MacOS malware nerds suspect it is an on-going MacSync malware campaign (or possibly Odyssey). I don't deal with MacOS malware so I don't know what this means.  - Person sends me weird goop they randomly got on Discord. It was multi-staged Electron JS malware. The main binary creates a Java Virtual Machine (for running Java code), while a secondary binary runs through four layers of obfuscation (file creating an obfuscated file, which contains an obfuscated file, which contains an obfuscated file) which ultimately delivers an AES128 encrypted payload. The final payload then performs interprocess communication with Java Virtual Machine the main process created. I have no idea what this malware is. It is partially vibe coded using Claude.  - Person sends me weird .exe. It turns out it's a Epic Games account creation spam tool. The spam tool was used for a Discord Nitro aggregation campaign. It would create Epic accounts in bulk to receive Discord Nitro for free (temporary promotion between Epic and Discord) which they then sold online for $5. A spammer DMd me and was frustrated I reverse engineered it and shared the decompiled code.  - Person DMs me. They got a weird e-mail that contained a heavily obfuscated .vbs file. However, the .vbs file was corrupted. I complained about it on social media and someone decided to debug and fix the malware payload for me. The .vbs ended up pulling a password protected .zip from DropBox. The password protected .zip file contained several different files such as a credential stealer and a Monero miner. It turns out this is a new and evolving malware campaign from FAUX#ELEVATE. Initially they targeted French people, however it appears they have changed targets.  - Person links me to No Man Sky's save editor website. This is a fake website which gives you a fake save editor. It is a .zip password protected with the string "goatfungus". The files inside of this .zip were a gigantic pain in the ass to reverse engineer due to it's anti-reverse engineer measures in place, anti-virtual machine components, and multi-staged payloads. It ended up being GachiLoader and KidKadi Loader. These malware campaigns coincide with a larger Rhadamanthys malware campaign.
I truthfully thought it would be malware. It is not. It is unironically exactly as it advertises. It is a Gmail spam tool thingy.

Chat, I'm flabbergasted.  I had a few people DM saying this account is spreading malware. They said the GitHub has a .exe which is suspicious.  I poked it with a stick.  It is, in fact, NOT malware.  gist.github.com/vxundergroun…
Look at this. I FUCKING TOLD YOU. Why do you mock the people who actively are trying to compromise you or your users?

I FUCKING KNEW IT.  I saved a file on my computer called "do_not_delete". It was on April 28th, 2026. Polymarket was mocking people for discussing whether or not Polymarket was compromised (in fairness, it wasn't), however Polymarket mocked Threat Actors, indirectly boasting their cybersecurity capabilities.  I publicly stated it was a bad idea to taunt Threat Actors because taunting them is a recipe for disaster, additionally they're a large organization with a large target on their back.  IT FUCKING HAPPENED. I TOLD YOU. I TOLD YOU.  Anyway, "told-you-so" aside, I am willing to gamble this is not the first time, or the last time, Polymarket or Polymarket users are targeted. This is only the beginning.  How do I start a Polymarket prediction thingy on Polymarket being compromised?
Cybersecurity researchers keep getting flagged and/or suspended on X.  Chat, it's only a matter of time for me. If I die, please collect pictures of cats in my honor.

Tangential to the AC debate, is the role McDonald's has played in bringing AC to the masses, especially in Asia. When McDonald's first came to Hong Kong in 1975, few other spaces available to the working class had AC.  The McDonald's were also clean, with much higher levels of hygiene, and people had to wait in line, and they became so popular, especially with the elderly and poor, that many establishments quickly copied them — and so they changed the culture, not only increasing overall levels of hygiene, but creating an expectation and demand for AC.

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-06-28 08:02 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 위협 환경은 AI 에이전트와 자동화된 코딩 도구를 악용한 공급망 공격이 급증하고 있으며, 공격자들은 신뢰받는 GitHub 저장소나 오픈AI의 조직 초대 기능을 활용해 보안 솔루션을 우회하고 있습니다. 특히 Signal 백업 복구 키 탈취와 같은 메신저 크리덴셜 타겟팅이 고도화되고 있으며, 클라우드 자격증명 탈취를 목적으로 한 악성 저장소 유도 공격이 빈번하게 발생하고 있습니다. 또한, AI 기반의 공격 기술이 MITRE ATT&CK 프레임워크와 결합하여 더욱 정교해지고 있어, 기업의 자동화된 인증 프로세스에 대한 재점검이 필수적인 시점입니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **Russian Intelligence Services**: Signal 백업 복구 키 탈취 및 메신저 크리덴셜 탈취 캠페인 수행 (TheHackerNews, BleepingComputer)
- **Gamaredon**: 터널링, Dead Drop, 신규 Alliance를 활용한 C&C 인프라 은닉 및 데이터 유출 (WeLiveSecurity)
- **Kimsuky**: 고객 문의를 위장한 스피어 피싱 공격으로 보안 담당자 타겟팅 (이스트시큐리티 알약)
- **CL-STA-1062**: 동남아시아 정부 및 주요 인프라 대상 TinyRCT 백도어 활용 스파이 활동 (Unit42)
- **Lazarus Group**: Kelp DAO LayerZero 브릿지 침해 및 약 $292M 규모의 가상화폐 탈취 (ThreatCluster)

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **Kimsuky**: 보안 담당자를 노린 스피어 피싱 공격 지속 확인.
- **공급망 공격**: 북한발 npm 악성 패키지(`chai-as-init`) 유포 사례 확인.
- **기타**: 트위터 내 한국발 DDoS 노드 탐지 보고 및 금융권 대상 피싱/Dropper 공격 증가 추세.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-20251**: Splunk Secure Gateway, CVSS 8.8, RCE 취약점 (악용 중)
- **CVE-2026-3227**: TP-Link Routers, OS Command Injection (PoC 공개)
- **CVE-2026-48907**: Joomla Content Editor, Critical (악용 중)
- **Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server**: 미패치 취약점, CISA 긴급 패치 권고 (악용 중)
- **Linux Kernel Vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-53312~53324)**: 다수의 NULL dereference 및 메모리 오버플로우 취약점 (패치 권고)

### 5. IOC 요약
- **도메인**: 없음 (뉴스 내 도메인 정보 미포함)
- **해시**: 없음 (뉴스 내 해시값 미포함)
- **CVE**: 
    - CVE-2026-20251 ([DarkWebInformer](https://twitter.com/DarkWebInformer/status/2070966091870859345#m))
    - CVE-2026-3227 ([DarkWebInformer](https://twitter.com/DarkWebInformer/status/2070671285990895695#m))
    - CVE-2026-48907 ([DarkWebInformer](https://twitter.com/DarkWebInformer/status/2070959081406201865#m))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **AI/자동화 도구 보안**: 개발 환경에서 사용하는 AI 코딩 도우미(Amazon Q 등)가 외부 저장소를 참조할 때의 권한을 제한하고, 신뢰할 수 없는 코드 저장소 접근을 차단하십시오.
- **메신저 보안 강화**: Signal 등 메신저의 백업 복구 키를 클라우드에 저장하지 않도록 설정하고, 다중 인증(MFA)을 활성화하여 크리덴셜 탈취에 대비하십시오.
- **공급망 패치**: CISA가 경고한 Cisco 취약점 및 Chrome 브라우저의 4개 Critical 취약점에 대해 즉시 패치를 적용하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
- **점수**: 8/10
- **근거**: AI 에이전트를 악용한 공급망 공격과 국가 배후 해킹 그룹의 메신저 타겟팅이 결합되어 실질적인 데이터 유출 피해가 지속적으로 발생하고 있음.
